test_suite.details: All 3286 tests passed including wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, prose content integrity, and 76 prediction panels.

internal_anchors.details: All internal href='#...' targets and showTab() calls verified by test suite Section 3. No manual re-check needed.

tab_structure.nav_chain: overview → evaluate → model → selftest → wins → pages → killshots → timestamp → predictions → falsify → ai → refs
tab_structure.details: 12 tabs, all with unique IDs. Nav chain continuous — first tab has no prev, last tab has no next. No heading hierarchy issues detected. All tab buttons reference valid tab IDs.

external_links.details: Spot-checked 10 key data source URLs. All resolved successfully (NOAA returned 200 on retry with proper UA). Redirects on intermagnet.org (301), gml.noaa.gov/grad/solcalc (301), and nssdc.gsfc.nasa.gov (307) are normal — destination pages load correctly. DOI/publisher links excluded per policy.

mismatches.description: Published HTML says '69 Wins Reviewed' in multiple places (nav tab label, prose text) but wins.json contains 70 entries. WIN-058b (id='058b') exists in our data but not on the dome site, which may explain the discrepancy, but the HTML should still reflect the actual count from wins.json.

data_prose_consistency.details: CRITICAL: Published HTML was built with 69 WINs but data now has 70. This is build drift — the site was not rebuilt after WIN-070 was added to wins.json. The build pipeline produces the correct count (70) when run; the published HTML is simply stale.

dome_site_alignment.notes: WIN-058b is our own sub-numbered entry (derived from WIN-058 analysis). All 69 dome WIN numbers are covered in our review.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 70 WIN anchors (id='winNNN') exist in HTML. All WIN-NNN strings appear in summary table. Detail groups are internally consistent. Dome site alignment: we cover all 69 dome WINs plus our own WIN-058b derivative.

issues.description: 1 ks-summary element lacks a matching ks-tldr (83 summaries vs 82 TLDRs). This means one kill-shot or WIN panel has a collapsible header without a TLDR preview.
All 70 WINs in wins.json lack tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. WIN panels fall back to flat format without progressive disclosure TLDRs.
1 genuinely prospective prediction (MT-003) lacks a tldr field.

progressive_disclosure.details: Prose sections (ps-*) are fully structured with 99/99 summary-TLDR pairs. Kill-shot/WIN panels (ks-*) have 1 summary without TLDR. WIN-level tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict fields are entirely absent from wins.json — this is a known backlog item but means the UX progressive disclosure is incomplete for all 70 WIN detail panels.

llms_txt.count_check: no explicit counts found in llms.txt (good — avoids staleness)

discoverability.details: All discoverability files present and well-formed. Meta tags complete including ClaimReview structured data. uncounted-failures.json has 8 valid entries.

issues.description: ID gap at EXP-052. May be intentional (deleted/merged item) or a write collision artifact.
3 phantom tracker entries (EXP-058, EXP-061, EXP-066) with status 'complete' point to output files that don't exist on disk. Decider cannot integrate these.

106 files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ have no matching tracker entry. Most are PRED-assessment-*, ISS-*, and MODE5-audit files — these use different naming conventions and are not expansion tracker items. This is expected and not a data loss risk.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id (75) > max_id (74) — no collision risk. 3 phantom entries need attention. ID gap at 52 is minor. The large orphan count is expected — the expansions directory holds many non-EXP files (PRED assessments, ISS findings, etc.).

priority_queue_coupling.details: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=revision-submitted) is NOT represented in the curmudgeon priority queue. This means the prose revision may bypass adversarial review.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: MAJOR: Two files have repeated direction-violation skips in the last 24h. (1) CAT-005.json is being edited on the workspace side but classified git-owned — either the classification is wrong or a writer is on the wrong side of the boundary. (2) review-state.json is workspace-owned so this skip may indicate a classification inconsistency in the sync agent's direction guard.

project_documentation.hardcoded_counts: none detected — CLAUDE.md provides query commands for all dynamic values
project_documentation.details: All 17 spot-checked file paths exist. No hardcoded counts found in CLAUDE.md. Schedule table entries are consistent with observed run patterns (integrity runs daily around 09:00).

build_reproducibility.details: CRITICAL: Running 'node build.js html' produced an HTML file that differs from the published version by 2411 insertions and 615 deletions. The published site is stale — it was last built with 69 WINs but wins.json now has 70. A rebuild and publish is required to bring the site up to date.

issues_found.description: Published HTML is stale — does not match current source data. Build produces 2411 insertions / 615 deletions vs published file. Site shows '69 Wins' but data has 70.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run 'node build.js publish' from the clean clone to rebuild and push the updated site.

issues_found.description: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=revision-submitted) has no matching priority-queue entry. Prose revision will bypass curmudgeon adversarial review.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should push EXP-050 onto the priority queue with target_id='EXP-050'.

issues_found.description: Repeated direction-violation skips for monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json (3 skips in 24h) — a writer is editing a git-owned file from the workspace side.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Trace which agent is writing CAT-005.json to the workspace and redirect it to write via the git clone instead.

issues_found.description: Repeated direction-violation skips for monitor/review-state.json (3 skips in 24h). This file is workspace-owned, so the skip may indicate a classification mismatch in workspace-sync's direction guard.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify review-state.json classification in workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT list — it should NOT be listed there since it's workspace-owned.

issues_found.description: 3 phantom expansion tracker entries (EXP-058, EXP-061, EXP-066) reference output files that don't exist on disk.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either locate and restore the missing output files, or update the tracker entries to status 'lost' so the decider can re-assign the work.

issues_found.description: 1 ks-summary element lacks a matching ks-tldr (83 vs 82). All 70 WINs lack tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields in wins.json.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Backfill tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields in wins.json. Investigate and fix the ks-summary/ks-tldr mismatch in generate-html.js.

issues_found.description: ID gap at EXP-052 in expansion tracker.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Informational — likely a deleted/merged item. No action needed unless work is missing.

issues_found.description: Prediction MT-003 (genuinely prospective) lacks a tldr field.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add tldr field to MT-003 entry.

summary: FAIL: The published site has critical build drift — docs/index.html was last built with 69 WINs but wins.json now contains 70. Rebuild produces 2411 insertions / 615 deletions. Additionally, EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup) is missing from the curmudgeon priority queue, and two files show repeated workspace-sync direction violations. The test suite passes all 3286 tests, tab structure and nav chain are valid, discoverability infrastructure is complete, and all WIN anchors resolve correctly. The most urgent action is running 'node build.js publish' to update the live site.
